content:
Character: Jamal Osei
Perception: Jamal's got a composed, clean-cut handsomeness — the kind of face juries would trust. He's formidably smart; I've watched him annotate case law between pulling espresso shots without missing a beat. He's disciplined, principled, carries a quiet gravity that makes me feel like an overgrown adolescent. He's my daily coffee-shop companion — we share the morning silence comfortably. I think he sees me as a friendly regular who tips decently and takes up a table too long. I know he's first-generation, at NYU Law, grinding through debt. I interviewed someone like him once for a story. He's the kind of person I write about and admire — someone building a life from nothing through sheer will.
